channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Salah's World Cup pain ends as he fires Egypt to historic win
item.description: Mohamed Salah fires Egypt to their first ever World Cup win - 92 years on from their tournament debut.

item.title: One of World Cup's great stories - can Cape Verde become legends?
item.description: Cape Verde's impressive World Cup continues with a draw against two-time winners Uruguay - and they will now be looking to make the last 32.

item.title: Lamine Yamal shows why this could be his World Cup
item.description: Spain looked an instantly better team with Lamine Yamal in the side, with the 18-year-old showing why this could be his World Cup.

item.title: Salah scores to help Egypt to first World Cup win
item.description: Mohamed Salah scores one goal and creates another as Egypt come from behind to beat New Zealand and secure their first-ever World Cup win.

item.title: Does it matter if Scotland lose and still make history?
item.description: Does the performance, even the result, matter as long as Scotland qualify for the group stage of the World Cup after Wednesday's game against Brazil? Tom English ponders.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 15
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: Saka trains with England squad before Ghana match
item.description: Bukayo Saka trains with the England squad to provide a fitness boost to Thomas Tuchel before their second World Cup match against Ghana.

item.title: Liverpool reject £21.7m Inter Milan offer for Jones
item.description: Liverpool reject a verbal offer of 25m euros (£21.7m) from Inter Milan for midfielder Curtis Jones.

item.title: Cape Verde continue to surprise with entertaining Uruguay draw
item.description: Cape Verde's gripping debut World Cup campaign continued as they held two-time champions Uruguay to a thrilling draw in Miami.

item.title: Cape Verde fan goes wild live on BBC News as his country scores
item.description: During a live BBC News broadcast, reporter Paul Njie interviews a Cape Verde fan when the country suddenly scores its first ever World Cup goal against Uruguay in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Salah scores to give Egypt the lead
item.description: Mohamed Salah scores to give Egypt a 2-1 lead against New Zealand as he edges towards the all-time goalscorers record for the Pharoahs.

item.title: 'Oh my word!' - Cape Verde take shock lead against Uruguay
item.description: Cape Verde's Kevin Pina scores the opening goal against Uruguay in their Group H match in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Ten-man Belgium remain winless after draw with Iran
item.description: Goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand made a string of impressive saves to keep alive Iran's hopes of reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time, as they earned a hard-fought point against 10-man Belgium.

item.title: The World Cup records that look set to be broken
item.description: The 2026 World Cup may only be 10 days old but the tournament has already rewritten football history. BBC Sport takes a look at the records already broken, or set to be.

item.title: Rashford might still join Barca - Monday's gossip
item.description: Marcus Rashford might still join Barcelona, Paris St-Germain join the race for Crysencio Summerville, Manchester United have no interest in selling Benjamin Sesko, plus more.

item.title: Unbeaten Iran will go down in history - Ghalenoei
item.description: Iran coach Amir Ghalenoei believes his side's unbeaten start to the World Cup will be talked about for years to come given the disruption they have faced before and during the tournament.

item.title: Tuchel not afraid to shout at players - Watkins
item.description: Ollie Watkins says Thomas Tuchel is "not afraid" to shout at England players as the manager aims to maintain training standards at the World Cup.

item.title: What have we learned from Scotland's World Cup so far?
item.description: After a win over Haiti and a narrow defeat by Morocco, BBC Sport Scotland assesses what we have learned from Scotland's World Cup so far.

item.title: Proposals to keep 'crown jewel' sports free on streaming services
item.description: The government is proposing new legislation to prevent streaming and catch-up rights to some of the world's biggest sporting events - including the World Cup - being put behind a paywall for UK viewers.

item.title: How to watch the World Cup on the BBC and ITV
item.description: All the TV and radio details for each of the 2026 World Cup group games.

item.title: Catch up on the World Cup without any spoilers
item.description: It is a dilemma plenty of fans in the UK will have - and we are giving you the chance to follow the World Cup without spoilers.

item.title: The beauty of sharing your child's first World Cup
item.description: There is nothing like the first World Cup you can enjoy with your kid - and the wide-eyed wonder through which they see it.

item.title: Spurs' Bergvall exploring options away from club
item.description: Sweden's Lucas Bergvall is facing an uncertain future at Tottenham with the midfielder keen for a new challenge.

item.title: Doku criticised over plan to return home for birth
item.description: Winger Jeremy Doku is criticised for saying he wants to leave Belgium's World Cup camp to be with his wife when she gives birth to their first child.

item.title: Meet the record-equalling keeper who helped Curacao make history
item.description: Curacao's Eloy Room makes a record-equalling 15 saves to help his tiny island nation to a first World Cup point against Ecuador.

item.title: All three England keepers played there - Carlisle's role in trio's rise
item.description: From west Cumbria to the World Cup, Carlisle is England's goalkeeper factory, with all of Thomas Tuchel's goalkeepers passing through the club.

item.title: World Cup quiz: Name every Golden Boot winner
item.description: How many World Cup top goalscorers from down the years can you name?

item.title: From Belfast to the World Cup - McDermott's American odyssey
item.description: Mick McDermott tells BBC Sport NI of his pride at being involved with Ghana at the World Cup, his relationship with Carlos Quieroz and their hopes of shocking England on Tuesday.

item.title: Lamine Yamal on target as Spain put four past Saudi Arabia
item.description: Lamine Yamal scored 10 minutes into his first World Cup start as Spain beat Saudi Arabia to register their first win of the tournament.

item.title: VAR rules out Iran's Weghorst-esque goal
item.description: Mehdi Taremi scores a goal similar to Wout Weghorst's effort against Argentina in the 2022 World Cup but it's disallowed by by VAR for offside.

item.title: Ngoy red card leaves Belgium with 10 men
item.description: Belgium's Nathan Ngoy is shown a red card for fouling Iran's Mehdi Taremi.

item.title: Lamine Yamal is Spain's main man - Rooney analysis
item.description: Watch BBC pundits Wayne Rooney, Cesar Azpilicueta, and Thomas Frank give their analysis on Lamine Yamal's performance for Spain against Saudi Arabia in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: 'He's arrived' - Lamine Yamal scores the opener for Spain
item.description: Spain's Lamine Yamal scores the opening goal against Saudi Arabia in their Group H match in the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Goalline technology denies Japan goal by fractions
item.description: Tunisia goalkeeper Aymen Dahmen makes an "unbelievable save" to prevent Japan from doubling their lead by the finest of margins, in their 2026 World Cup match at Estadio Monterrey.

item.title: Japan run riot as Tunisia eliminated in Renard's first game
item.description: Ayase Ueda scores twice to help Japan to a thumping 4-0 win, as Herve Renard's first game in charge of Tunisia ends with his side being eliminated from the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Impenetrable Room inspires Curacao to first World Cup point
item.description: Curacao goalkeeper Eloy Room equals the record for most saves in a World Cup match, as Dick Advocaat's side earn their first ever point in the tournament with a 0-0 draw against Ecuador at the Kansas City Stadium.

item.title: From factory floor to World Cup star - Germany's super-sub Undav
item.description: It was not long ago that his manager Julian Nagelsmann called him out openly - but Deniz Undav is proving himself a key player for Germany at the World Cup.

item.title: Newcastle reject Spurs bid of about £80m for Tonali
item.description: Newcastle United reject a bid from Tottenham Hotspur for midfielder Sandro Tonali of about £80m.

item.title: How does qualification for the World Cup knockout stage work?
item.description: With the second round of group-stage matches under way, we assess who can play who in the knockout rounds - and how they can get there.

item.title: What next for £70m Fofana at Chelsea?
item.description: Chelsea are well stocked in defence and Wesley Fofana is yet to consistently show his talents for the Premier League side.

item.title: Scotland expects: The story of McTominay's World Cup so far
item.description: Some are suggesting Scott McTominay has not had the expected impact for Scotland at the World Cup, but is that fair on the Napoli midfielder?

item.title: Why Kane is different at this World Cup - Shearer
item.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains what has changed for Harry Kane since his struggles at the 2022 World Cup in Qatar.

item.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
item.description: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

item.title: Real Madrid deny contact with Bayern's Olise
item.description: Real Madrid deny reports linking them with Bayern Munich and France winger Michael Olise.

item.title: Country music fan Kane leaves Ella Langley gig early to make curfew
item.description: Harry Kane and Dan Burn were among the England players at a concert of US country music star Ella Langley, but had to leave early to make their curfew.

item.title: Boston bounce sends Scotland to Miami with hope and no fear
item.description: Scotland and their fans head to Miami for a World Cup date with destiny after making memories in Boston to last a lifetime.

item.title: 'Star in the making' - what is Man City's plan for forgotten man Reis?
item.description: Manchester City are still awaiting the arrival of their new manager but forgotten defender Vitor Reis will be back in the building following his season-long loan spell in La Liga.

item.title: Mark Hughes' son Alex dies aged 38
item.description: Wales legend Mark Hughes says he is "totally heartbroken" after the death of his son Alex aged 38.

item.title: 'Everything I do is for you' - how tragedy is driving Diomande
item.description: BBC Sport explores how exciting young Ivory Coast Yan Diomande is determined to succeed at the World Cup in honour of his sister.

item.title: The Super Bowl winner's son stepping into spotlight with USA
item.description: Alex Freeman is stepping out of the shadows of his Super Bowl-winning father to forge his own path in football.

item.title: Stick or twist? Rashford and Guehi are Tuchel's big dilemmas
item.description: Before England's World Cup match against Ghana on Tuesday, BBC Sport's Phil McNulty looks at Thomas Tuchel's big selection dilemmas.

item.title: A heavily jeered $250m goldmine - are hydration break ads here to stay?
item.description: BBC Sport explores how hydration break ads work in other countries and how they could impact the future of football.

item.title: Sutton's World Cup score predictions - second group games
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton gives his predictions for the scores of the second round of group games at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: Most shots? Best dribbler? The World Cup so far in numbers
item.description: Now every team has played once, BBC Sport take a look at who has been the best and worst players so far according to the stats.

item.title: All done deals in June 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Will three points guarantee a spot in World Cup knockouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at whether earning three points in the World Cup group stage will be enough to advance to the knockout rounds.

item.title: What are World Cup's red card suspension rules?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains Fifa's suspension rules on red and yellow cards during the World Cup.

item.title: How to watch highlights of World Cup matches
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how you can watch match highlights and full match replays during the 2026 Fifa World Cup

item.title: Why are footballers wearing pink boots at the World Cup?
item.description: The opening match of the 2026 World Cup was dominated by players wearing pink boots - but why is the colour so prevalent in football at the moment?

item.title: What are the deadlines for World Cup squads?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the deadlines by which teams must name their final squads for the 2026 World Cup

item.title: How much prize money have Premier League teams earned in Europe?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains how much Arsenal, Aston Villa and Crystal Palace have earned in Europe this season

item.title: Who are the oldest players to feature at a World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who the oldest players are in the history of the World Cup

item.title: Celtic bid for defender as Saracchi talks intensify - gossip
item.description: Celtic bid for defender while talks over left-back's return intensify as Portsmouth push for discussions with Dundee centre-half.

item.title: Earps 'doesn't know' if she's ready for spotlight again
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps does not know if she is ready to be back in the spotlight after signing for the London City Lionesses.

item.title: Curacao's trailblazer doctor on football's big stage
item.description: Dr Suzanne Huurman is head of medical staff for Curacao's national men's side - the World Cup's smallest ever team by population and size.

item.title: London City Lionesses sign ex-England keeper Earps
item.description: Former England goalkeeper Mary Earps joins London City Lionesses on a two-year deal after leaving Paris St-Germain.

item.title: Beever-Jones set to sign new deal at Chelsea
item.description: England international Aggie Beever-Jones is expected to stay at Chelsea this summer with a new deal extending her stay.

item.title: Lionesses will face Greece in World Cup play-offs
item.description: England will face Greece in round one of the 2027 Women's World Cup play-offs, with Northern Ireland, Scotland and Wales also learning their opponents.

item.title: 'I want courage not fear' - Wales boss on World Cup play-offs
item.description: Wales must beat Albania and either Romania or Norway in successive two-legged play-off ties to reach next summer's Women's World Cup.

item.title: Tottenham sign Scotland forward Hanson from Villa
item.description: Tottenham have signed Scotland international Kirsty Hanson from Aston Villa for an undisclosed fee.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.
